findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement Score
findings.description: The User Engagement Score reflects the overall engagement level of users with the platform, combining various interaction metrics to provide a holistic view of user activity.
assumptions: User engagement is a key driver of retention and revenue.
recommendations: Regularly monitor and analyze the engagement score to identify trends.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Content Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Content CTR measures the percentage of users who click on recommended content, indicating the effectiveness of the recommendation engine.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better content relevance and user satisfaction.
recommendations: Aim for a 20% improvement in CTR within the next 60 days.

metadata.measurement_method: tracked through user interactions with content

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: User Retention Rate (30 Days)
findings.description: The User Retention Rate measures the percentage of users who return to the platform within 30 days, serving as a critical indicator of user satisfaction and product value.
assumptions: Retention is essential for long-term growth and profitability.
recommendations: Ensure retention does not fall below 40%.

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap: User Behavior Tracking
findings.description: Current data collection does not adequately capture detailed user behavior such as browsing history, click patterns, and time-on-page metrics, which are essential for improving personalization.
assumptions: Comprehensive user behavior data is necessary for effective profiling.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics.

recommendations: Develop and implement a robust user behavior tracking pipeline.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing for Recommendation Engine
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different recommendation algorithms to measure their impact on Content CTR and user engagement.
assumptions: Different algorithms may yield varying levels of user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Target: 20% improvement in content CTR within 60 days.

recommendations: Set up A/B tests with a minimum sample size of 1,000 users per variant.

summary: The analysis identifies a North Star metric focused on user engagement, supported by primary KPIs such as Content CTR and guardrail metrics like User Retention Rate. Gaps in user behavior tracking instrumentation were noted, alongside recommendations for A/B testing to enhance the recommendation engine's effectiveness.
